name: Association of Muslim Lawyers (AML)
description: Established UK network of Muslim lawyers (solicitors + barristers). Member-led, not-for-profit. Private member directory.
rationale: Largest & longest-established Muslim lawyer network in the UK. Critical anchor for consortium.

name: Muslim Lawyers Action Group (MLAG)
description: Activist-oriented Muslim lawyer network focused on civil liberties and Prevent-related casework. Private membership.
rationale: Complements AML — strong on civil-liberties casework relevant to AskAdil's hate-crime track.

name: Muslim Lawyers' Hub
description: Newer networking & mentoring hub founded ~2022 by Farzana Abdullah. Focus on junior lawyers, diversity, career pathways.
rationale: Younger lawyers, cultural competence, strong LinkedIn presence — good for consortium's next-gen strand.

name: International Ahmadiyya Muslim Lawyers Association (IAMLA)
description: Global Ahmadiyya Muslim lawyers association. UK chapter active.
rationale: Broadens community representation — Ahmadiyya community often faces distinct legal issues (religious persecution cases).

name: Society of Asian Lawyers (SAL)
description: Pan-Asian network (includes many Muslim lawyers) — long-established, broad professional network.
rationale: Not exclusively Muslim but major overlap — expands consortium reach without diluting focus.

name: Muslim Lawyer UK / Muslim Solicitors
description: Referral network pairing Muslim clients with 'partner lawyers within 20 minutes of their home or business'. Public-facing; membership not published.
rationale: Already operating as a consumer referral network — most direct analogue to what AskAdil does.

name: SolicitorConnect — Islamic & Sharia Law
description: General solicitor directory with an Islamic/Sharia law category. Commercial listings.
rationale: Less strategic — listing rather than community. Include to cover completeness; lower priority.
